draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers low-contrast, minimal color palettes with reduced visual noise for focused work environments
facade.label: Dark monochrome interface
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward dark mode, desaturated color schemes, and reduced visual complexity in the AI workspace
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer brighter, more colorful, or higher-contrast interfaces that feel more energetic or visually distinct

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer metric-focused layouts (KPIs in visual blocks) over narrative summaries
facade.label: Numbers in cards
facade.acceptImplies: Emphasize card-based dashboards showing discrete stats, progress bars, live counts—scannable at a glance
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward contextual prose, trend stories, and explanations embedded in prose—users want the why, not just the what

facade.hypothesis: The user gravitates toward layered, dimensional interfaces that feel sculptural rather than flat—suggesting comfort with subtle visual hierarchy and organic visual richness.
facade.label: Soft shadows and depth
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace feels handcrafted, warm, with tactile micro-interactions and gradual visual transitions
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace leans crisp, minimal, immediate—flat surfaces, high contrast, instant readability over mood

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers low-contrast, minimal color palettes with reduced visual noise for focused work environments
evidence.implication: Lean toward dark mode, desaturated color schemes, and reduced visual complexity in the AI workspace

facade.hypothesis: An AI workspace benefits from fluid, animated state changes over instant snaps — reducing cognitive load and creating a sense of continuous flow.
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize easing curves, cross-fades, and staged reveals. Motion should guide attention and smooth context shifts.
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant state changes, snappy interactions, and no-motion feedback. Every input gets immediate visual confirmation without delay.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, deliberate transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: An AI workspace benefits from persistent left navigation paired with card-based content areas that feel lightweight and movable
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. content fluidity — does the user prefer stable anchoring or floating/modal-like interactions?
facade.label: Sidebar with floating cards
facade.acceptImplies: Favor persistent side navigation + card-based layouts in future probes; explore card sizing, stacking, and drag-ability
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward full-screen tabs, list-based layouts, or top navigation; avoid fixed sidebars in next iterations

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer warm, approachable language over corporate formality
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into personality, casual language, human-centered copy throughout the product
facade.rejectImplies: Maintain professional distance, formal terminology, business-standard voice

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer strategic pops of color against dark backgrounds to create visual hierarchy and guide attention in a focused workspace, rather than fully desaturated interfaces
facade.axisTargeted: Color saturation and accent strategy within dark interfaces
facade.label: Bright accent colors
facade.acceptImplies: Incorporate vibrant accent colors (neon, electric blue, bright cyan) sparingly against the dark monochrome base to highlight CTAs, active states, and key data points
facade.rejectImplies: Maintain fully desaturated, truly monochromatic dark interface with no color accents—reinforcing that low-contrast minimalism extends to complete color absence, not strategic highlights

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
